issues.comment: `isConditionalPasskeysEnabled()` is called with no arguments, but the only definition in the parent class `UsernamePasswordForm` requires a `UserModel` parameter: `isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(UserModel user)`. There is no zero-argument overload, so this will fail to compile. The call should be `isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(context.getUser())`.

`isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(UserModel user)` requires `user != null`, which inverts the passkey display logic from the old code. The old code set up passkeys when `user == null` (initial login page), but the new code via `isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(context.getUser())` only sets them up when `user != null`. This breaks the initial login flow: passkeys won't be shown on the initial login page. The condition should allow passkeys when user is null (or when user has passkey credentials configured).
